markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games' latest delay of Grand Theft Auto VI to November 19, 2026, has extended the window for major cultural and tech milestones to occur first, shaping trader focus on high-profile releases and announcements. Following the shift from an earlier May 2026 target—itself a postponement from 2025—the extra months for polish have boosted implied probabilities around anticipated drops like a new Playboi Carti or Rihanna album and the launch of GPT-6, each trading near even odds amid active volume. Take-Two's upcoming earnings updates and any fresh Rockstar communications on development progress remain key near-term catalysts that could influence momentum, while the extended timeline underscores how Rockstar prioritizes quality over speed in one of gaming's biggest launches.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent mistrials in Harvey Weinstein's third New York rape retrial, declared May 15, 2026, after the jury deadlocked on the Jessica Mann charge, combined with ongoing appeals of his 2022 California conviction carrying a 16-year sentence, have driven trader consensus toward No Prison Time at 79.1%. Weinstein, now 74, faces repeated procedural setbacks in New York following the 2024 overturn of his original 23-year sentence, while his legal team pursues challenges that could reduce or vacate time served. These developments underscore the case's protracted nature amid #MeToo-era scrutiny, with sentencing still pending on the 2025 partial conviction but overshadowed by appeals and the producer's health concerns. Upcoming catalysts include potential further retrials or appellate rulings that could clarify total exposure.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vegas Golden Knights hold a 2-1 lead in the 2026 Stanley Cup Final against the Carolina Hurricanes following three overtime thrillers marked by multiple comebacks and power-play swings. The Golden Knights' resilience in erasing deficits, combined with Shea Theodore's double-overtime winner in Game 3 and home-ice positioning for the next contest, underpins their 62.7% implied probability as traders weigh series momentum and playoff experience. Carolina's regular-season dominance with 113 points and strong Eastern Conference standing supports its 37.5% share, though recent road results and even-strength adjustments have tempered sentiment. The tightly contested series, with both clubs demonstrating elite depth and goaltending, explains the current pricing gap without decisive separation.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: The New York Knicks' 2-0 series lead in the 2026 NBA Finals, secured via road victories in Games 1 and 2 against the San Antonio Spurs, has driven their dominant position in the championship market. The Knicks, who swept the Eastern Conference playoffs and enter Game 3 at Madison Square Garden as series favorites, have shifted from pre-Finals underdogs to clear frontrunners according to trader consensus. Their recent form, including an extended postseason win streak, underpins the elevated implied probability. Meanwhile, the Spurs, who advanced through the West behind a young core, face elimination pressure after dropping the opening pair on home court, leaving them with limited margin for error in the best-of-seven matchup.

markets.question: Will MegaETH perform an airdrop by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if MegaETH launches a token and performs an airdrop by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market "locked" tokens or non-swappable tokens will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes". Airdrops of NFTs will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market is on-chain information and official information from MegaETH, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The tight clustering among top contenders for the 2026 FIFA World Cup reflects broad parity in squad depth and recent international pedigree across Europe’s elite sides. Spain and France sit narrowly ahead on the strength of consistent tournament performances, balanced attacking options, and defensive organization, while England, Portugal, Argentina as defending champions, and Brazil remain within striking distance due to star power and historical knockout success. Minor late injury concerns and finalized rosters have produced limited shifts in trader consensus, underscoring how evenly matched these nations appear heading into group-stage play.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping's consolidated control over the Chinese Communist Party, military, and state apparatus underpins the 92.7% trader consensus that he will remain in power through the end of 2026. No verified internal challenges, health issues, or factional realignments have surfaced in 2026, despite prior military personnel shifts. Public appearances, including a May 2026 summit in Beijing and ongoing policy directives, reflect uninterrupted leadership. With no designated successor in place, analysts anticipate Xi will pursue a fourth term at the 21st Party Congress in autumn 2027, well after the market's resolution window. Only unprecedented events such as sudden illness or major elite upheaval could alter this trajectory before then.

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom holds the top position in the wide-open 2028 Democratic field at roughly 25 percent because of his executive record as California governor, established national fundraising network, and frequent media visibility positioning him for broader voter appeal. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez draws support from progressives focused on economic and climate priorities, while Kamala Harris benefits from prior vice-presidential and nominee name recognition. Other contenders such as Jon Ossoff and Josh Shapiro reflect Senate or gubernatorial strengths in key states. No candidates have formally declared, and early positioning centers on travel to Southern states ahead of the primary calendar, with outcomes likely to hinge on 2026 midterm results, subsequent polling trends, and eventual primary dynamics before any single contender consolidates backing.
